{"key_event":"La Violencia and authoritarian National Front transition","reasoning":"State power rose slightly because siege rule, militarized coercion, coffee-boom resources, public works, and the National Front settlement strengthened executive and administrative capacity, even as rural violence exposed weak monopoly of force and legitimacy. Society power fell because terror, censorship, closed elections, party exclusion, and repression damaged open civic organization, despite women’s suffrage and the 1957 civic strike showing renewed urban and elite mobilization.","society_change":-0.7,"society_power":5.4,"state_change":0.2,"state_power":6.6}
